items.title: US strikes Iran after Trump slams 'foolish' ceasefire breach

children.content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps vows a "swift and decisive" response, according to Iranian state television, claiming it had repelled a US attack on Sirik Island, on the shores of the Strait of Hormuz.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump stands at a podium with US flags in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump stands at a podium with US flags in the background.

items.title: Why hasn't the global economy completely collapsed?

children.content: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

image.altText: Donald Trump smiles while looking to the side.

items.title: Shock results from peptide test reveal 'life-threatening' ingredients

children.content: Doctors warn of an "enormous" risk of overdose after testing of a popular unapproved weight loss peptide reveals the product had double the strength of what was listed on its label.

image.altText: A vial of liquid sitting on a table with a label saying 'retatrutide 10mg'

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

articles.title: Heavy rain to soak Australia's east as un-El-Niño-like weather continues

children.content: For an El Niño year, 2026 is so far the wettest on record for Australia, and an influx of tropical moisture is about to deliver another round of widespread rain.

image.altText: a rainbow and heavy dark clouds over the water at port macquarie

items.title: Defence home loan subsidy to change after veterans left worse off

children.content: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

items.title: Venezuela's quake began with a 'soft shake' and then the lights went out

children.content: As a rare one-two punch of earthquakes hit Venezuela this week, residents in the capital Caracas watched in horror as buildings shook and were sent "pancaking" before their eyes.

items.title: Farewell: 27 things Virginia Trioli learned at the ABC

children.content: After almost 40 years in Australian journalism, 27 of them at the ABC, I am now in possession of enough mistakes to claim a small amount of experience.

items.title: At 80, John Williamson is ready to hang up his guitar

children.content: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

articles.title: Dembélé suspends time as France's favouritism heightens

articles.title: Tony Popovic hits back at discussions about his World Cup selections

items.title: How this WA couple cut their build time in half amid housing crisis

children.content: It takes 12 to 18 months on average to build a home in Western Australia — but with non-brick frame construction, the home building timeline could be cut by up to half.

items.title: While teaching about pancreatic cancer, John realised he ticked every box

items.title: Art 'like medicine' for accountant turned artist after brain injury
